title: How Long Does It Take to Design an App?
excerpt: App design timelines depend on scope, complexity, and how prepared you are when the project starts. This post provides a realistic breakdown of how long the design phase takes for different types of app projects.
content:
One of the first questions clients ask when discussing a mobile app project is: "When can we launch?" Before anyone can answer that, you need to understand how long the design phase alone will take.

Here are realistic benchmarks.

Simple App (5–10 Screens)

Design Timeline: 3–6 weeks

A focused app with a limited feature set — think a simple booking tool, directory, or utility app — can move through design relatively quickly. Discovery, wireframes, and high-fidelity screens for a small scope typically takes 3–6 weeks.

Mid-Complexity App (10–25 Screens)

Design Timeline: 6–12 weeks

Most business apps fall in this range. Multiple user flows, onboarding screens, dashboards, settings, and profile management all need to be designed carefully and tested in prototype form. Expect 6–12 weeks for thorough design work.

Complex Consumer or Enterprise App (25+ Screens)

Design Timeline: 3–6+ months

Apps with complex logic, multiple user roles, admin dashboards, or high-volume transaction flows require extensive UX research, architecture planning, and iterative design. Rushing this phase is one of the most common causes of app failure.

Design vs. Development Timelines

Remember: design is only one part of the total app timeline. After design comes development, QA testing, and app store submission — each adding additional weeks or months.

Total app launch timelines (design + development):
  • Simple app: 2–4 months
  • Mid-complexity: 4–8 months
  • Complex app: 8–18+ months

Factors That Affect Design Timeline

  • Clarity of brief and product requirements
  • Availability and speed of client feedback
  • Complexity of user flows and interactions
  • Number of revision rounds
  • Need for user testing and iteration
